reviews.review_msg: He's a very good lecturer.  He's at his best when in discussion with students, so speak up in class.  Tests are fair and pretty much exactly what we did in class.
Nice guy

Dr. Mellow is such sweet man and prof. Despite his name, he is not mellow. He is particular and buzzes around class giving you millions of handouts to keep. Often his class consists of looking at the handouts, reading through them and highlighting, underlining and circling instructions. This was fine for in-person, online however, it was very dull.

Dr. Mellow is the most patient professor I have had at SFU. He answers every question we have. Even something he had said in class a lot of times. When you are not able to come to class, he will give you the handout and notes next class. He is the person who is really sweet, please choose the course if he teaches. It is worth.

Very clear explanations, but you don't really learn much. He basically gives you all the answers you need for his midterm and final exam. Very strict on writing, which is odd because linguists are supposed to be descriptivists. Regardless, I am glad that I got to encounter Dean during my undergrad and wish he spoke more about his own work.

I have no idea why such morphologist are so willing to make every of his student a newspaper writer. Course itself is easy, and this professor who is the only authority of morphology at SFU claims that he likes every of his course is a writing course. If you like to teach a W course go teach it, not torturing students by bunch of papers.
What a kind & smart prof. He is not patronizing at all like so many profs are. Clear expectations and consistent marker. Very excited about ling. If anything, could get more specific at times.

Dean's a total sweetheart who really tries to help students. He's really clear on his expectations. You always know what he wants of you. His lectures can be a wee bit boring at times, but he tries so hard you can't really fault him.
really smart guy.
Really nice guy, very easy course. I slept through half the classes and ended up with 96%. His exams are very slack, hopefully he toughens them up in the future.

Trevor will forever be my favorite professor. He is very clear with what he expects, and he is very reasonable with the content of the tests and the assignments, as he makes sure to relate everything to what is discussed in lecture. His office hours are very helpful, and he is super approachable! Take at least one course with him in your degree!
John's a nice enough guy, but he's not the best teacher. He posts videos in addition to his lectures, and you're better off just watching those at home and saving yourself the trip to campus.

First day of class stated "I mean if you use AI for your homework you could only get a B at best", so dont use it. 
Homework is worth 66% of the mark, and on the biweekly homeworks sometimes the students would get 0 feedback on their homework. Students complained in class and he simply said "I dont have time to give feedback for everyone"...

he deserves his rating. does not have clear criteria at all. gives you a grade without specific explanation. classes boring. does not even use canvas to remind things and assign grades. makes you frustrated about morphology. please definitely take 323 with another prof

He is a knowledgeable professor, even though sometimes it took time for me to understand the content, he was willing to help and reply emails fast. Read textbook really helped me. Teaching style did not suit me, but he is a good professor, just ask him question if you are confused. Quizzes are NOT easy.

John is a very difficult dude. Considering taking him for a course? Sit the semester out and wait for a different professor to teach it. Seriously. You won't get much support from this guy, and you'll learn much more from a textbook.

All over the place, Does not know what he is doing. Often unsure how to analyze the data, but expects us to know how to. Made us do Analytical summaries, but did not teach us prior knowledge.  Basically told us: Life is challenging, so I will give you more challenges. Not helpful during office hours . Leaves right after class. Textbook is better

Not bad at all. The course mainly focuses on 2 aspects, word schema and word syntax. His handwriting appears not good, however, the lec notes he provided were good enough. Textbook reading is recommended.

His basis for marking is unclear. You never know why you got a certain mark on an exam or homework. He doesn't like when you visit his office hours. Sometimes, you will get a 100 on the homework, and other times you will get a 70, but you will never know why.
He is very emotional. When he is happy, he is good. Overall I think he is not a good prof. The exams are not doable. Essays are very hard.
No Comments

My favourite Linguistics professor at SFU.  He is very thorough AND encourages thinking further than what he's teaching.  His class was a lot of fun.  The final project was the most fun I've had with any project.
He's not bad!
No Comments

Very nice, prof you can tell she cares about her students and is very passionate, though 3 hours is a long lecture and is hard to get through. Her exams are quite difficult I found so make sure to study a ton but she does give bonus marks. Keep in mind that she does a lecture before the exam and that content will be on the exam.

Makes no sense in the lectures; she goes on and on with examples, but doesn't actually teach the concept. I can tell she's passionate, but she isn't great at expressing it. Her time management is not great either, made 2 assignments due the night before our final, which makes no sense as professors should give time to students to study on their own

She likes to get to know your name- that's AWESOME. But... the class material is difficult to understand as there are only a few words on slides. Lots of charts. Read the textbook for the class as it's helpful!!
Her exams are bad and her lectures are boring, her ratings should not be this high

Eva is super passionate about the topic and really wants her students to succeed. Her 3 hour lectures can feel a bit long, and while the homework practice is helpful, I do wish there was a clear answer key. Her exams are quite difficult despite being mostly m/c - make sure you really study and attend office hours if you're confused.
